description: Moderate recovery, poor sleep, heavy busyness with a fragmented, scattered calendar (many short meetings rather than long blocks). Micro format, grace theme.

request.reason: A scattered, under-slept day calls for the theme of renewed-daily mercy rather than achievement.

data.text: Because of the LORD's loving devotion we are not consumed, for His compassions never fail. They are new every morning; great is Your faithfulness.

verses.fetchedText: Because of the LORD's loving devotion we are not consumed, for His compassions never fail. They are new every morning; great is Your faithfulness.

devotionalOutput.devotionalBody: Six minutes between meetings, and you started the day short on sleep. That's not a personal failing — it's just where your body is today. The prophet Jeremiah wrote from real ruin, not a tidy schedule: 'Because of the LORD's loving devotion we are not consumed, for His compassions never fail. They are new every morning.' New — not saved up from yesterday's better rest, not owed because you earned it. Just freely renewed, this morning, for exactly the scattered day you're having. You don't need a full night's sleep to receive grace. You just need this minute.
devotionalOutput.cardSummary: His mercies are new this morning, scattered day and all. Lamentations 3:22-23.
devotionalOutput.prayer: Lord, my morning was fragmented and my sleep was short. Meet me in these few minutes with mercy I didn't earn and don't need to earn. Renew me for what's still ahead today. Amen.
devotionalOutput.actionStep: Before your next call starts, silently say 'new mercy, not new performance' once — let it reset your posture for the next twenty minutes.
